dominants.dominant_desc: The Basif with the first skill of stoning the
Arlot

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Dyrroth's primary strength is his passive, which shreds physical defense, and his ultimate, which deals massive burst damage. A skilled Arlott player can counter.

counters.counter_desc: This is arguably the strongest counter to Arlott. Phoveus's entire kit is designed to punish heroes who dash and blink. Arlott's reliance on his Vengeance dash makes him the perfect target for Phoveus's ultimate, which automatically triggers and deals damage every time a hero blinks or dashes.

dominants.dominant_desc: This combination of items, emblems, and a well-timed skill rotation allows you to capitalize on a more aggressive playstyle and is a great way to use Arlott as a burst assassin. While it sacrifices some survivability, the potential for one-shotting key enemy heroes makes it a very rewarding and effective build in the right hands.

emblems.emblem_desc: The effectiveness of a build often depends on the player's skill, team composition, and the specific match-up. While Festival of Blood is generally considered the top choice for Arlott due to its synergy with his sustain-focused playstyle, Lethal Ignition can be a very powerful alternative.

equips.equip_desc: While the standard build for Arlott focuses on a hybrid of damage and tankiness for better sustain, a full-damage, or "one-shot," build is a valid and highly effective strategy, especially for experienced players. This build turns Arlott into a lethal assassin who can quickly eliminate squishy targets.

hero.hero_overview: A damage and burst build for Arlott focuses on maximizing his physical attack, penetration, and cooldown reduction to enable him to one-shot or quickly burst down squishy heroes.
hero.hero_strength: Dominant Early to Mid-Game. Once he gets his first core items like Hunter Strike and Blade of the Heptaseas, he can easily bully most enemy laners and get a gold advantage. His ultimate, Final Slash, has a wide area of effect and can knock up multiple enemies.
hero.hero_weakness: Vulnerable to Crowd Control (CC): Arlott's entire kit is based on chaining his skills. If an enemy can stun, suppress, or silence him, his combo is immediately broken. Heroes with heavy CC like Franco, Minsitthar, or Kaja can completely shut him down and make him an easy target.  Lack of Escape: While his second skill, Vengeance, provides great mobility, it is a target-locked dash.

spell.spell_desc: You can use your ultimate and then immediately use Flicker to blink into the enemy's backline, surprising them and knocking up their squishy damage dealers.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: This duo combo is super good, infinite 2nd skill potential when Zhuxin casts her ultimate with its lift.
Fredrinn also enables Arlott's passive a lot quicker. The potential wombo combo for this duo is high! And you can add Zhuxin to the mix for ultimate CC trio!

cooperates.cooperate_desc: With Arlott's passive that marks every enemy that is cc'd while near him this pairs incredibly well with tigriel's 2nd and 3rd skill which consists of a push, airborne and sucking or stun that allows you to further increase the number of times you get to spam your 2nd skill.

counters.counter_desc: With Arlott's 2nd skill being a dash skill this allows it to be easily shut downed by minsitthar's ult and putting you at a huge disadvantage since you will lose your main damage dealing skill.

dominants.dominant_desc: In team fights you have the option to either keep the whole enemy team from acting with your incredible cc or to pick off those potential damage dealers in the backlines.

emblems.emblem_desc: An almost full fighter emblem would allow you to fully make use of Arlott's huge lifesteal from his 2nd skill but with the added defense of tenacity your survivability is higher even at low hp.

equips.equip_desc: When playing Arlott always prioritize in building his core item war axe to get that valuable spell vamp and that additional trie damage, also getting queens wings this early in the game allows you to pick fights with the enemy exp laner with a much higher spell vamp and survivability chance.

hero.hero_overview: Arlott is an incredibly annoying enemy to face in the exp lane since even as low as level 2 he can already spam his 2nd skill and dash all over the place to confuse your enemy.
hero.hero_strength: Arlott excels in mobility and regen since even a good and uninterrupted combo could even take your hp from 5% to 100% easily.
hero.hero_weakness: Arlott may excel against low mobility exp laners or to those prone to cc but to those who can pin Arlott down can easily out damage or out sustain him.

spell.spell_desc: Petrify is a handy battle spell especially in the early game since you wouldn't have that much cc to make use of your combos and spam your 2nd skill, but with this you not only have an ability to pin opponents but a means of activating your passive marks.

dominants.dominant_desc: Try to play safe and farm level 4. Try to poke your laning enemy with skill 2 as much as you can, so your jungler can finish off when he rotate your lane. The key is to farm level 4 before turtle spawn.
Help your jungler to secure the objectives. Try to initiate the fight if there is opportunity. Use your second skill and ultimate to zone the enemy jungler.

data.title: Bush

dominants.dominant_desc:
Stun and Displacement: His passive, Demon Gaze, marks enemies with a "bright mark" after CC (like slows or knockups), which allows him to dash and deal AoE damage with Vengeance (Skill 2).

AoE Displacement (Ultimate): His ultimate Final Slash pushes all enemies in a direction, setting up for big combos and displacing enemies for better positioning in team fights.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Physical Attack: Increases the damage of basic attacks and skills.

Best for split-push assassins

data.recommend: It will be helpful for Beginners

spell.spell_desc:
1.Stun Enemies (AoE Effect)

Effect: Stuns enemies for 0.8 seconds in a small area around the hero.

Benefit: This brief stun can interrupt enemy skills (like channeling ultimates) and disrupt enemy formations.



2. Bonus Magic Damage

Effect: Deals a small amount of magic damage to all enemies hit.

Benefit: This additional damage can finish off low-health enemies or provide extra burst damage in fights.
